items.title: This Fund Sold $6 Million of a Mid-Cap ETF Even as Shares Rose 13%
items.author: Jonathan Ponciano
items.description: Lakeridge Wealth Management sold 47,314 shares of the First Trust Mid Cap Core AlphaDEX Fund (FNX) worth approximately $5.92 million in Q4, reducing its position to 1.47% of assets. Despite the sale, FNX shares have risen 13.48% over the past year, though they lag the S&P 500 by 3.51 percentage points. The sale reflects portfolio discipline and suggests mid-cap factor exposure is a satellite position rather than a core conviction.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The fund showed solid 13.48% returns over the past year, but the institutional sale suggests it is being trimmed as a non-core position. The fund slightly underperforms the S&P 500 and carries a Bronze Medalist rating from Morningstar, indicating respectable but cyclical performance. The sale reflects portfolio discipline rather than disappointment.
Mentioned as a top holding in Lakeridge's portfolio ($11.83 million, 5.44% of AUM) and recommended by The Motley Fool. No specific news or sentiment drivers are discussed in the article.
